baseline_dominance.justification: The construction claims five axes of baseline dominance, but Tier 3 attacks materially weaken several. The rfc7662_advocate (Attack 3) shows RFC 8693 bulk pre-issuance + offline JWT achieves zero AS roundtrips at request time, neutralizing Axis 1 as stated. The rfc7662_advocate (Attack 4) demonstrates BBS+ with 64 per-bit attributes can express the bitwise predicate with runtime-adaptive verifier-chosen disclosure sets, refuting Axis 2's claim that 'bitwise AND has no BBS+ extension.' The spiffe_engineer (Attack 4) shows SPIFFE + ZK attestor plugin achieves AS-blindness without a new protocol. The genuine residual differentiator is circuit-enforced cumulative implication closure over a private bitmask — real but narrower than claimed. The construction is preferable to baseline but the baseline can approximate most claimed properties with extra configuration, fitting the 1-point definition.

formal_security_argument.justification: The construction provides an explicit threat model with three named games (SCOPE-FORGE, SCOPE-HIDE, AS-BLIND), four named cryptographic assumptions (A1-A4), and reduction sketches. This is substantive. However, Tier 3 attacks expose critical gaps: (1) The cryptographer (Attack 1) and every other persona identify that scopeCommitment is a permanent cross-session fingerprint, breaking the multi-RS privacy claim — SCOPE-HIDE is single-shot and does not capture session unlinkability. (2) The cryptographer (Attack 2) shows SCOPE-HIDE is brute-force broken because the effective permission space is ~256 values and the adversary knows all other Poseidon5 inputs, recovering the full bitmask in sub-millisecond. (3) The cryptographer (Attack 4) shows the Groth16 trusted setup assumption is missing — an adversarial AS that participated in the pot16.ptau ceremony can forge proofs, making 'adversarial-AS resilience' circular. (4) The spiffe_engineer (Attack 1) shows adaptive chosen-mask bit recovery across 64 sessions fully recovers the bitmask via accept/reject side-channel. The threat model is stated but assumptions are incomplete and multiple reductions fail under realistic multi-session adversaries.

implementability.justification: The construction maps directly to the existing AgentPolicy circuit already implemented in circuits/src/AgentPolicy.circom. Every gadget (Num2Bits, Poseidon5, EdDSAPoseidonVerifier, BinaryMerkleRoot, LessThan) is a current Bolyra primitive. Circuit cost is estimated at ~15,500 constraints, well within the pot16.ptau ceiling of 65,536. Proving time targets are stated (Groth16 rapidsnark <0.5s, snarkjs <5s, PLONK <5s). Proof size is 192 bytes (Groth16) or 768 bytes (PLONK). On-chain verification cost is ~220K gas via EIP-196/197 precompiles. No new primitives are required. This fits Phase 1 budget.

adversarial_survival.justification: Tier 3 found multiple attacks the construction cannot answer without fundamental changes: (1) scopeCommitment cross-session fingerprint — identified by all five personas — directly contradicts the §7 multi-RS privacy claim and fixing it breaks the delegation chain seed property. (2) Brute-force bitmask recovery via small permission space (cryptographer Attack 2) — 256 Poseidon evaluations recover the full bitmask, breaking SCOPE-HIDE entirely. (3) Adaptive chosen-mask bit recovery (spiffe_engineer Attack 1) — 64 sequential probes fully recover the bitmask via accept/reject side-channel, and the single-shot SCOPE-HIDE game does not capture this. (4) Trusted setup circularity (cryptographer Attack 4) — adversarial-AS resilience claim fails when AS participated in the ceremony. (5) Revocation latency (auth0_pm Attack 2) — 30-root buffer with unspecified flush cadence is structurally incompatible with the credit union regulatory scenario. These are not mitigated attacks — they are unanswered breaks in the core claims.

scenario_fit.justification: The construction names a concrete deployment (Navy Federal CU, 13M members, three resource servers with specific permission requirements) and cites NCUA examination guidelines. However, Tier 3 attacks expose the scenario as hypothetical rather than load-bearing: the cu_ciso (Attack 1) shows ZK opacity contradicts GLBA Safeguards Rule and FFIEC CAT audit trail requirements — the RS cannot log what it cannot see. The auth0_pm (Attack 4) shows no SOC 2, no cyber insurance, no SLA — vendor risk committee rejects on day one. The cu_ciso (Attack 4) shows the 30-root buffer creates a 60-second availability cliff incompatible with core processor SLAs. The scenario is named with a real stakeholder but the regulatory and operational requirements of that stakeholder contradict the construction's properties, making it hypothetical rather than load-bearing.

gaps: scopeCommitment must be randomized per session (e.g., Poseidon3(permissionBitmask, credentialCommitment, sessionNonce)) to eliminate the cross-RS fingerprint — and the delegation chain seed mechanism must be redesigned to not depend on a stable scopeCommitment
SCOPE-HIDE game must be upgraded to an adaptive multi-query game; the construction needs a blinding salt in scopeCommitment to prevent brute-force recovery over the small (~256) valid bitmask space

Adaptive chosen-mask bit recovery via accept/reject side-channel must be addressed — either the agent must be able to produce a proof for any mask (padding unset bits with noise) or the protocol must rate-limit or bind masks to prevent sequential probing

Trusted setup must be explicitly modeled: either add 'honest trusted setup' as assumption A5 and acknowledge the limitation, or mandate PLONK with security arguments under PLONK-specific assumptions, or require a publicly verifiable MPC ceremony for pot16.ptau
Revocation mechanism must specify root update cadence and an emergency invalidation path (e.g., on-chain revocation bitmap) with wall-clock time guarantees compatible with regulatory requirements
Audit trail reconciliation: define how nullifierHash + agentMerkleRoot map to a human-readable FFIEC-CAT-compliant audit log, or explicitly scope the construction to non-regulated deployments
Operator key custody model must be specified (HSM class, rotation cadence, compromise recovery) since the operator EdDSA key is the enrollment trust anchor replacing the AS

Baseline dominance claim must be narrowed: drop 'zero AS roundtrips' as a differentiator (RFC 8693 pre-issuance matches it), drop 'bitwise predicates are inexpressible in BBS+' (per-bit attribute model works), and foreground the genuine novel property: circuit-enforced cumulative implication closure over a private bitmask
